trigger.reason: Google DeepMind's Gemini Spark agentic assistant reaching Mac is a major capability expansion with direct threat to productivity software, customer-service tools, and automation-adjacent sectors.

primary_event.headline: Gemini Spark, Google's agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
primary_event.summary: Google has expanded its 24/7 agentic assistant Gemini Spark to macOS with real-time tracking and support for more apps. This platform expansion brings autonomous agent capabilities to a major computing segment and directly competes with productivity software, automation tools, and customer-service automation vendors.

secondary_events.headline: Meta, like SpaceX, looks to turn excess AI compute into cash
Trump drops restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos and Fable models

discovery.trigger_acknowledgment: Analyzed Google DeepMind's Gemini Spark agentic assistant launch on macOS, targeting productivity software, customer-service automation, RPA, workflow automation, and scheduling software as at-risk sectors.
discovery.run_summary: The trigger's blast radius theoretically hits horizontal productivity/automation software, but today's tape shows a curious bifurcation: vertical workflow SaaS names (PAYC, PCTY, MANH, TYL, NCNO) actually rallied, suggesting the market is (correctly) distinguishing regulated/embedded workflow moats from horizontal agent-vulnerable software. The clean sympathy-fade shorts are the semi-caps and DOCN, which have no plausible connection to a consumer agentic-assistant release. Green candidates are skipped as they don't fit the fade-buy pattern.

discoveries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly frames AI/ML as a growth vector for DOCN: 'A growing part of our business involves AI/ML products and services, and we are devoting significant resources to develop and deploy such strategies.' No language suggesting agentic assistants replace IaaS/PaaS demand for developers. DOCN is an AI inference/compute provider to SMB developers, not a productivity tool.
discoveries.thesis: Gemini Spark is a consumer/prosumer Mac agent — it has zero business-model overlap with DOCN's SMB/developer cloud infrastructure. If anything, more agentic AI usage drives more inference compute demand, which DOCN monetizes. The sharp drop looks like generic 'AI news = bad for smaller cloud' reflex selling, unsupported by the filings.
discoveries.what_confirms: DOCN's AI inference product revenue trajectory in next Q reporting; commentary from management on GenAI agent workloads driving GPU/inference demand.
discoveries.what_kills: A hyperscaler pricing action targeting SMB developers, or DOCN customer concentration in productivity-SaaS end-markets that themselves get hit by agents (unlikely per the customer base description).
discoveries.what_to_learn: Commodity/SMB cloud names get thrown into every 'AI is disruptive' basket regardless of whether the specific AI release competes with their offering — this is a repeatable sympathy-fade pattern when the trigger is a consumer AI product.
discoveries.catalyst: Gemini Spark, Google's agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: DOCN is on curated AI-adjacent list; the trigger's 'productivity/automation' sectors do not include IaaS, making the sharp drop mechanically hard to justify from the announcement.
research_pointers: Check DOCN's disclosed AI/ML revenue mix and QoQ growth rate
Verify no concurrent 8-K or preannouncement on the drop day
Compare DOCN move vs. hyperscaler moves (AMZN, GOOGL, MSFT) — if hyperscalers rallied and DOCN dropped, that's the sympathy-fade signature

discoveries.name: Photronics, Inc.
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K describes PLAB as a photomask supplier: 'We sell substantially all of our photomasks to designers and manufacturers of IC and FPD electronic devices.' No AI-competition risk factor whatsoever. Every AI accelerator chip requires photomasks — a Google DeepMind consumer agent release has zero mechanical link to photomask demand, which is if anything a beneficiary of AI capex.
discoveries.thesis: PLAB is picks-and-shovels for chip manufacturing. A Google agent-on-Mac release is neutral-to-positive for AI chip demand and therefore for photomasks. The drop is pure sector-basket sympathy with no fundamental linkage.
discoveries.what_confirms: Continued semi-cap sector recovery in coming sessions; strong TSMC/Samsung capex commentary; PLAB customer concentration in leading-edge nodes tied to AI chip production.
discoveries.what_kills: A concrete semiconductor demand warning; PLAB China exposure hit by new export controls; a large customer loss disclosure.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Semi-cap picks-and-shovels names get lumped into 'AI-adjacent' selloffs on lab news, but their revenue depends on wafer starts, not on which lab shipped what. Track this decoupling.
discoveries.catalyst: Gemini Spark, Google's agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: PLAB is on the curated AI-adjacent semis basket; the trigger sector list doesn't include semis at all, making the drop a pure basket-sympathy artifact.
research_pointers: PLAB revenue mix by node (leading-edge vs mature)
Any recent guidance revisions or customer-specific commentary
Cross-check with ONTO/AEIS moves to confirm sector-wide sympathy pattern

discoveries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
discoveries.filings_evidence: 10-K explicitly names AI capex as a demand tailwind: 'infrastructure investments in artificial intelligence (AI) have increased substantially, which is driving significant demand increases in the Data Center Computing market. We accelerated investments to increase capacity and make upgrades to support higher demand.' AEIS is a direct AI-buildout beneficiary through power delivery for fabs and data centers.
discoveries.thesis: AEIS provides power conversion for AI data centers and semiconductor fabs — the release of an agentic assistant on Mac increases, not decreases, the compute infrastructure demand that AEIS serves. The drop is entirely sector-sympathy, mechanically backwards to the actual capex thesis.
discoveries.what_confirms: Data Center Computing segment growth in next reporting; continued hyperscaler capex announcements; management commentary on AI-driven demand.
discoveries.what_kills: Data center segment margin compression that offsets volume; loss of key hyperscaler design win; concrete slowdown signal from a major customer.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Power infrastructure names benefit from AI compute buildout regardless of which lab ships what consumer product — the sympathy-fade is mechanically inverted for these names.
discoveries.catalyst: Gemini Spark, Google's agentic assistant, is now available on Mac
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: AEIS is on curated AI-adjacent semis-picks-and-shovels basket; trigger sectors don't include power/infrastructure, making the drop pure basket sympathy.
research_pointers: AEIS Data Center Computing segment revenue trajectory and margins
Hyperscaler customer concentration and design-win pipeline
Whether the drop coincided with any broader semi-equipment weakness or was specifically an AEIS-only signal

skipped.reason: Semi-cap picks-and-shovels with clean AI-capex beneficiary read, but concurrent Rigaku acquisition disclosure in latest 10-Q introduces name-specific noise; prefer the cleaner PLAB/AEIS setups for the same thesis.
Positive move — not a fade-buy candidate. Market appears to be correctly distinguishing regulated payroll from agent-vulnerable software.
